prompt: How many evaluations were submitted for each course?

inputData.question: How many evaluations were submitted for each course?

response.bodyRaw:
{"status":"success","steps":{"input":{"question":"How many evaluations were submitted for each course?"},"sql":{"payload":{"question":"How many evaluations were submitted for each course?"},"startedAt":1771803860700,"status":"success","output":{"reasoning":"Count course_evaluations per course by joining courses -> course_sections -> enrollments -> course_evaluations. Use LEFT JOINs to include courses with zero evaluations and deterministic ordering by count desc then code.","sql":"SELECT c.id AS course_id,
       c.code AS course_code,
       c.name AS course_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT ce.id) AS evaluation_count
FROM public.courses c
LEFT JOIN public.course_sections cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN public.enrollments e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
LEFT JOIN public.course_evaluations ce ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY evaluation_count DESC, c.code ASC;","assumptions":["Evaluations are linked to courses via course_sections -> enrollments -> course_evaluations (enrollment_id -> enrollments.id, enrollments.course_section_id -> course_sections.id, course_sections.course_id -> courses.id).","Return should include courses with zero evaluations.","Counting distinct course_evaluations.id to avoid double-counting in case of join multiplicity."]},"endedAt":1771803892868},"run-raw-sql":{"payload":{"sql":"SELECT c.id AS course_id,
       c.code AS course_code,
       c.name AS course_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT ce.id) AS evaluation_count
FROM public.courses c
LEFT JOIN public.course_sections cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN public.enrollments e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
LEFT JOIN public.course_evaluations ce ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY evaluation_count DESC, c.code ASC;"},"startedAt":1771803892873,"status":"success","output":{"ok":true,"sql":"SELECT c.id AS course_id,

GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY evaluation_count DESC, c.code resultRaw.sql:
SELECT c.id AS course_id,
       c.code AS course_code,
       c.name AS course_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT ce.id) AS evaluation_count
FROM public.courses c
LEFT JOIN public.course_sections cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN public.enrollments e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
LEFT JOIN public.course_evaluations ce ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY evaluation_count DESC, c.code ASC;

result.sql:
SELECT c.id AS course_id,
       c.code AS course_code,
       c.name AS course_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT ce.id) AS evaluation_count
FROM public.courses c
LEFT JOIN public.course_sections cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN public.enrollments e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
LEFT JOIN public.course_evaluations ce ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY evaluation_count DESC, c.code ASC;
